name: Damietta Governorate (Egypt)

colors.symbolism: the Mediterranean Sea and the Damietta branch of the Nile

description: A blue field charged in the centre with the white-and-yellow emblem of Damietta Governorate.
history: The flag of Damietta Governorate displays the governorate emblem on a deep blue field, adopted as part of the system of governorate flags introduced in the 2000s. Damietta is a Mediterranean port at the mouth of the eastern (Damietta) branch of the Nile.

features.symbolism: the Mediterranean Sea and the Damietta branch of the Nile that meet at the governorate
the emblem of Damietta Governorate, a port at the mouth of the Damietta branch of the Nile, evoking the city's maritime and historical character
